text.title: Three Core VUI Design Principles
text.summary: Three testable principles — Simplicity, Natural Conversation, Context Awareness — with concrete do/don't examples and an evaluator rubric for automated checking.

rule.statement: One idea per system turn; no redundant phrasing, units, timestamps, or location details unless explicitly requested.
rule.rationale: Voice is ephemeral — users cannot re-read. Dense responses cause cognitive overload; research shows comprehension drops sharply when spoken output exceeds two clauses.

example.code: System: "Your order is confirmed for delivery tomorrow."

example.note: Redundant detail — date, time window, timezone all unprompted
example.code: System: "Your order has been successfully processed and scheduled for delivery on January 20th at a time between 9 AM and 5 PM in your local timezone."

rule.statement: Sound like a person: use contractions, conversational rhythm, and offer one helpful follow-up after completing a task.
rule.rationale: Formal language signals a machine and breaks the conversational contract; users disengage or revert to terse commands that hurt NLU coverage.

rule.statement: Ban stop-list filler phrases from system prompts: "I'd be happy to", "Just to confirm", "I have successfully", "Currently".
rule.rationale: LLMs default to verbose, apologetic tone and will not self-edit reliably; the stop-list must be enforced via system prompt or post-processing.

example.code: User: "What's the weather?" System: "It's 72 and sunny. Should I tell you about tomorrow?"
User: "What's the weather?" System: "The current temperature is 72 degrees Fahrenheit with sunny conditions. Humidity is 45 percent."

rule.statement: Use prior turns implicitly; never ask users to repeat information they already provided in the same session.
rule.rationale: Forgetting prior context is the leading source of VUI frustration; users interpret it as incompetence and abandon flows.

rule.statement: Track explicit dialog-state slots (intent, last_entity, pending_confirmation) in agent context — never reconstruct state from utterance history alone.
rule.rationale: Reconstructing state from text is fragile and drifts; explicit slot tracking makes state deterministic and auditable.

example.code: User: "Set an alarm for 7 AM" System: "Alarm set for 7 AM tomorrow." User: "Make it 6:30" System: "Updated to 6:30 AM."

rule.statement: Encode the three principles as a JSON rubric with scores 0-3; use it in agent critic passes instead of free-form review.
rule.rationale: Agents apply structured scoring more reliably than prose instructions; a numeric rubric also enables trend tracking across iterations.
